D4 Colorimetry L3 Apply Reference

The Colour-Matching Experiment

slide the test wavelength and watch one primary go negative — added back to the test side to force a match

To match a pure wavelength, observers mixed red, green, and blue primaries on one half of a split field. Some colours are too saturated, so a primary must be added to the test half instead.

Slide the test wavelength. The split field shows the test on the left and the primary mix on the right; when the red amount runs negative, that red is spilled onto the test side — the origin of the negative lobe plotted below.
